storyInfo: Enciodes works to maintain order under pressure, while Enya searches for the cause of the Catastrophe in the Hall of Writings. With the Great Elder's advice, she decides to embark on a Pilgrimage to calm the people.

attributes.content: 'When the winter nights were long and there was no food to be found, She walked into the forests to look for seeds...'
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: No, not this one either.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: The word 'Catastrophe' doesn't even exist in the Old Kjeragi language. Is it possible that we truly have never been struck by a Catastrophe before?
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Or, maybe the ancient people couldn't tell Catastrophes from normal disasters, and mixed their records with others...
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: 'The snowmelt flooded field and pasture... the crops fell to an untimely winter tide...'
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: No, no... none of these sound like Catastrophe.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: I remember there's another story that she used to tell...
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: 'When the cold winter night froze the Heart of Karlan, the enemy from afar came across the ice.'
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: 'The warrior stood vigil with weapon in hand. At dusk, he saw a terrifying abomination in the mirror-like ice... Radsorn.'
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: 'The warrior ran back to his clan to warn the others, but...'
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: No one but the warrior could see the creature. Nobody believed his words. And so the warrior began to live in fear.'
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: Elder Adso? When did you...?
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: I came after morning prayers. I did not wish to disturb you, so I remained quiet... but I could not resist the urge to speak when you recited a familiar section of the scriptures.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: I apologize for disturbing you.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: It's alright... I just thought you were still handling matters of court.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: The light is dim. From Enya's vantage point, sitting on a high ladder, the Great Elder's robes seem to blend into the color of the floor.
She cannot see his expression.
You must be very busy after taking over as Great Elder. Is everything going well?
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: You think too highly of an old man like me, Great Saintess. I have spent most of my life here in the Hall of Writings, and I hardly ever step out of the Vine-Bead Court, not to mention dealing with matters of court...
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: My only claim to this position is my age, and apart from that, I can only do my best...
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: You're too modest. I haven't had many chances to speak to you, but I understand that you are a man of great learning.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: You flatter me... the more I study the tomes, the more conscious I am of my own ignorance. We all are, compared to Her.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: But I heard that you had been studying the scriptures for two days and nights without rest, so I came to see if I could share the burden.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: It's good timing. I... also have some questions that I would like to discuss with you.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: She closes the scroll and climbs down.
The old wooden ladder creaks as it faithfully connects knowledge in high places and the silent ground.
Enya walks out from amongst the bookshelves with scrolls in her arms, sitting down by the spacious table that is already covered in open scrolls.
This is what I've compiled over the past two days...
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: I searched for any and all records of unusual disasters in Kjerag, but I have yet to find any evidence of a Catastrophe ever having occurred in our borders.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: And I still haven't been able to find an explanation as to why this Catastrophe chose this moment to strike, when there has been nothing particularly unusual about this year's winter.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: The girl looks up from the scrolls to the Great Elder.
However, the old man seems uninterested in the scriptures lying before him. He bows his head, squinting with the sagged eyelids that lie below his white hair.
You... do not to seem approve of the studies that I have pursued.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: No... I dare not be so impudent...
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: You are seeking the truth of the Catastrophe, the massive thing that has descended before us, the fear incarnate.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: Like digging a path through a mountain, or building a bridge over a river... your actions are undoubtedly logical.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: But to search the entirety of Kjerag's history, every little tribulation within the fragments... it is an ordeal akin to searching for a grain of salt in the snow by candlelight.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: I'm not afraid of ordeals... I must find an explanation for this Catastrophe... I must.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: I understand. You worry for Kjerag, and take pity on those affected by the disaster... and I can feel there is some other anxious emotion driving you.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: But perhaps this anxiety has made you short-sighted.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: I've never asked you in private, Great Elder Adso...
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: What do you think of this Catastrophe that has struck Kjerag?
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: I must admit that my knowledge is shallow, and unable to explain the reason behind this Catastrophe.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: But I agree with what you said a few days ago...
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: Kjeragandr never promised Her people that they would be safe from the threat of Catastrophe.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: Indeed, Kjeragandr has never made any promises to Her people. On the contrary, the scriptures often emphasized that we must not validate Her existence through 'miracles'...
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: We believe in Her because we believe She is good, that She embodies all that is good. We follow Her way such that we can be our better selves.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: I agree with you...
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: But I fear the public will not respond well to such an interpretation.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Indeed. 'Reason and insight are a pair of golden shoes that will take you far, but only if your feet can fit them.'
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: The day may come when your path of truth is validated. But it is not today.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: Then, what do we do?
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: What do we do... that is a question I do not have an answer for.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: But you were reciting the story of Radsorn. Let us continue.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: The book on the table records a conflict that occurred over water sources.
It is an old story, a common refrain in many civilizations. The Great Elder reaches out and pulls another book from under it.
Dust intertwines with the scent of charcoal from the fireplace, like a piece of amber hovering in the air.
'The warrior searched far and wide for a way to fight the monster, but even Kjeragandr could not help the warrior kill a monster that could not be seen.'
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: 'And so She led the warrior back to the frozen lake, and She spoke to him, saying, 'The others cannot fight the monster, for they do not see it. Only you may find the way to defeat it.''
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: 'The warrior was enlightened, the Heart of Karlan began to melt, and winter was driven out by spring.'
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: 'Kjeragandr granted Her people the final blessing—Her tears froze the enemy, becoming three eternally frozen peaks.'
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: The first story of the Book of Home in the Kjeragandr. It has been subject to various interpretations by scholars of generations.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: The Heart of Karlan likely refers to Lake Silberneherze, and some believe that this warrior, who heeded the words of Kjeragandr, was the very archetype of the Saintesses.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Opinions differ on the 'enemy that came across the ice'. As for the 'monster that could not be seen'...
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: —Radsorn.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: A word that has long lost its true meaning, and one of Kjerag's most famous bedtime stories.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: Parents tell this story to children who refuse to go to sleep, before tucking them back in.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: The name is so widespread that most people have forgotten that 'Radsorn' was not an incantation or meaningless babble, but a word with a clear, specific meaning.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: Many words lose their meaning over time.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: At one point, 'Radsorn' was believed to be a loanword that came to Kjerag a thousand years ago, of the same origin as the word 'Catastrophe'.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: If I recall correctly, your work in the Hall of Writings involves decoding and translating imported words that have lost their original meanings...
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Research that has, fortunately, borne fruit.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: The word 'Radsorn' has two parts: the short, stopped syllable 'Rad', and the stiff, sibilant syllable 'Sorn'.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: The fireplace flickers as the Great Elder draws a line across the page with his finger, leaving a crease of shadow that fades away in a second.
Cutting in half a mysterious abomination, the likes of which has never been seen before.
The word 'Sorn' is not hard to understand. It translates roughly to 'something terrifying', or 'unknown monster'.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: It comes from Old Ursine dialects spoken in Kjerag, which are still used by some of the older generation. It serves as the anchor for our exploration.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: 'Rad' comes from Victorian, sharing an etymology with the word 'Radlithe', which means 'blind' or 'short-sighted'.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: Five centuries ago, a famous playwright from County Hillock penned a word in his play, The Rose of Shah.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: 'Radlyth', which means 'unseeable, 'a place of darkness'.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Yes. So you've seen the manuscript, and the comments on it.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: Unfortunately, I was young at the time I left those comments, and my knowledge deficient.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: I have pored over many pages in the years that followed, which granted me sufficient knowledge to discover and address the errors of my past...
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: The room is silent except for the sound of pen writing on paper, soft as the sound of a tiny bug crawling across the page.
The word reads 'Radsorn', the two etymologically different roots separated from each other, with a circle around 'Rad'.
It is, indeed, a place that is unseeable, a place of darkness that one cannot discover on one's own. It means 'the heart'.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: Then the meaning of 'Radsorn' is...
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: The terrifying thing in the heart of man.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Exactly.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: The warrior saw his inner demons upon the lake...
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Others could not see it, because no one else can know your own fears. And Kjeragandr was powerless as well, because the only thing that can defeat one's inner demons is oneself...
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: What is truly terrifying is not the disaster that we can see and feel, but the monsters in our hearts...
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: You are wise, Saintess.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: The sages realized this a thousand years ago... Now, this Catastrophe that has struck Kjerag, is also a 'Radsorn' which only Kjerag can see.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: But now, there is no Kjeragandr to lead the lost warrior...
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Why do you say so?
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: Kjeragandr could not help the people fight their inner demons, for She is absolutely good, without the flaws of man. The darkness that clouds the people's hearts does not exist in Her.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: So She chose a Saintess from among the mortals, and bestowed upon her the power to fight 'Radsorn'.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: Only she is always at Kjeragandr's side. The snow washes her eyes, her piety judged by ice. She hears the guidance of Kjeragandr, and cherishes the love of the people.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: Only she can dig open ice and soil, and make a path for the people of Kjerag out of their heart's maze, such that 'Kjeragandr' will return to us.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: That is what the people of Kjerag have believed for centuries... Only the Saintess may atone on their behalf, on behalf of the children of Kjeragandr.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: The Great Elder places his hands on his knees and bows his head in an imploring gesture.
The Saintess of the Vine-Bead Court sits on the other side of the candle. The flame flickers, and the wick grows long.
You are proposing...
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: A Pilgrimage.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: It is time for the noon prayers. I'm afraid I must take my leave, Saintess.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: One moment, Elder Adso.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Saintess...
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: Something you said caught my attention...
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: You said Kjeragandr has never made any promises to Her people, and that she does not rely on miracles to demonstrate Her existence to Her believers.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Do you... Do you believe that Kjeragandr doesn't exist?
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: No...
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: Of course Kjeragandr exists.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: Look at our bodies, our faces, and hear our own language... Every tree and grass, rock and brick in Kjerag is proof of Kjeragandr's existence.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: Of course, some hope for a divine spectacle from our god to affirm their own faith.
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: But if a 'god' truly stood before us, answering our every call, what need would remain for faith?
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: This is the first thing that every believer should know. For you to bring this up now...
attributes.name: Adso

attributes.content: Never mind... I misspoke.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: The old man silently bows and leaves.
He disappears through the door, like a speck of dust brushed off a page.
I see you've beaten me here.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Did the two of you summon me to discuss disaster relief?
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: What the hell is this, Enciodes?
attributes.name: Arctosz

attributes.content: Arctosz tosses a package bearing the Victorian crest on the table.
A supply package, containing a day's ready-to-eat rations, as well as emergency medicine.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Don't play the fool! I'm asking, why are there so many Victorians in Kjerag at a time like this!
attributes.name: Arctosz

attributes.content: Their reporters are babbling all over the place, soldiers dressed in worker's garb everywhere. I saw more Victorians than burdenbeasts on my way here!
attributes.name: Arctosz

attributes.content: Don't tell me you don't know about this. I'm well aware that Karlan Trade has been handing this stuff out!
attributes.name: Arctosz

attributes.content: The Vine-Bead Court never enacted a ban on Victorians entering Kjerag.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Besides, Victoria's help has been substantial in our present situation.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: What would it do to Kjerag's international reputation if we rejected a country's assistance, for no reason, at a time like this? Let alone expelling our visitors.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Some bloody help! Do you think we can't overcome this Catastrophe on our own, without those hypocrites pretending to be good neighbors?
attributes.name: Arctosz

attributes.content: The teacup on the table ripples, sending part of its contents splashing onto the wooden surface.
It faintly reflects the heads of the three clans, like three mountains of different heights.
Besides, what kind of help can the Victorians bring?
attributes.name: Arctosz

attributes.content: Oh, I remember—that man Harold 'helped', didn't he?
attributes.name: Arctosz

attributes.content: Drinking with our people, chatting them up, going into the pens, treating the beasts and delivering new foals... He made the burdenbeasts fat and plump.
attributes.name: Arctosz

attributes.content: People say, he's a Victorian, but he's not a bad guy.
attributes.name: Arctosz

attributes.content: And what happened? What did he do at Lake Silbernherze?
attributes.name: Arctosz

attributes.content: Here I thought you were supposed to be the smart one, Enciodes, but you couldn't even learn that lesson?
attributes.name: Arctosz

attributes.content: I'm telling you, no matter what the Silverashes and Browntails think, not one Victorian will set foot on Paleroche land from this day forth.
attributes.name: Arctosz

attributes.content: I don't care about your 'political goals' or 'international reputation'. I won't let the Victorians have their way on Kjerag soil!
attributes.name: Arctosz

attributes.content: Calm down, Arctosz. Are we going to fight amongst ourselves before we've dealt with the foreigners?
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: Then... What do you think, Ratatos?
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: The fireplace continues to crackle. Ratatos's voice is calm, the dark rings around her eyes showing the toll that the past few days have taken on her.
This time, I'm with Arctosz.
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: You've dealt with the Victorians the most. You know their ways, especially at a time like this.
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: Victoria does not show kindness to Kjerag for no reason. The aid could turn into a poison pill at any time.
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: All the chaos we've seen over the past few years, what part of it had nothing to do with foreigners? You, of all people, should understand the risks.
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: I completely agree, Ratatos.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: But we must not forget that, while stagnation may be the least risky course of action at present, it is also the surest way to doom.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Sending people at a time like this, Victoria is testing us. They are hoping that we will fall apart on our own.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: It has taken so long and so much for Kjerag to finally see the results of reform. To close our doors again at this junction would be to lay waste to all that we've done over the years.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: You're set on dealing with the Victorians?
attributes.name: Arctosz

attributes.content: You have my word.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Karlan Trade has taken responsibility for establishing contact with other nations. We will handle Kjerag's relationship with Victoria.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: How can you be so sure?
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: As you said, Victoria has never given up on exploiting us.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Have they ever succeeded, though?
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: That look again... like you're always in control, always so sure of yourself.
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: Kjerag is changing. Not just the changes that you've brought about, but also the changes brought about by Catastrophe, and by progress.
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: Nothing stays the same forever. That's true of your past successes too.
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: And it's true of the great powers' lofty perch.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: By Kjeragandr, I hope you know what you're doing...
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: In the distance, the evening prayer bell tolls at the Vine-Bead Court, echoing amongst the pillars.
Sir Enciodes.
attributes.name: Vine-Bead Court Cleric

attributes.content: The Saintess requests your presence at the Hall of Lectures.
attributes.name: Vine-Bead Court Cleric

attributes.content: The Saintess?
attributes.name: Arctosz

attributes.content: It seems we'll have to continue this conversation another day.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Even if we have not reached an accord, do not forget it was the decision of the Vine-Bead Court to open up Kjerag to foreign dialogues.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: To cross the line now is to defy the Vine-Bead Court and the Saintess herself.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Hmph... don't try to keep my head down with some tough words.
attributes.name: Arctosz

attributes.content: But remember this: My eye is open, and I won't allow anyone to do anything that will harm this land.
attributes.name: Arctosz

attributes.content: There's no length I won't go to protect Kjerag.
attributes.name: Arctosz

attributes.content: Do remember, Sir Enciodes, that your decisions affect more than just your domain and your company. This whole nation will be affected.
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: I hope Kjerag will not pay the price for your pride...
attributes.name: Ratatos

attributes.content: No one can make Kjerag 'pay', as long our people remain united.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Excuse me.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Your visitor is here, Saintess.
attributes.name: Handmaiden

attributes.content: Wait at the door.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Do not allow anyone to enter without my permission.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Yes, Saintess.
attributes.name: Handmaiden

attributes.content: For what purpose do you summon me, Saintess?
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Nothing important. I just realized that it has been some time since your presence last graced the Hall of Lectures.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: If I am being charged with impiety, then I have no defense.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: I will come to hear your lectures each week, once the current crisis has passed.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: I know you are a busy man, but I have rarely seen you so fatigued.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: I take it the matters at hand have been troubling.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: I'm sure you understand that the people have been restless in the aftermath of the Catastrophe. There is much to be done.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: But the Vine-Bead Court will only grow busier... take care.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Enciodes gestures under his eyes with his finger. Enya sees it, but says nothing.
The fire burns bright in the fireplace, its warmth reaching the eyebrows and noses of the Silverash siblings, warming their pale and alike complexions.
They are not looking at one another, but towards a solemn statue.
I saw Arctosz today. He looked out of sorts as he greeted me. Even Ratatos did not seem her normal self.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: It won't be the first time the three of you have fought, but a situation like this... I haven't seen in a long time.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: What happened?
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Temporal matters that you need not worry yourself about.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Then please tell me—what are the 'temporal matters' that trouble Kjerag?
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Is it the Victorians?
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Yes and no.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: They immediately sent a large relief effort to Kjerag, as soon as news about the Catastrophe got out.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: The Columbians also learned about the incident at the observation station, and threatened to send troops to protect and evacuate their scientists.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: They are paying close attention to this Catastrophe, each for their own reasons.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: So they understand Kjerag better than we think... they know well what this Catastrophe means to Kjerag.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: The politicians smell chaos, and see it as a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to extend their influence into Kjerag.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: We know that Victoria has been doing far more within our borders for a long time. They have simply stopped trying to hide it.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: I have a good guess as to who is behind Victoria's movements. Her plot is not hard to decipher; it is simply the instincts of a politician.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: If I was in her shoes, I'd agree it would be but another chess piece to move—
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: You know I don't like it when you get like that.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: I apologize. I spoke out of turn in this holy place.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Why do you keep putting yourself in the plotter's seat, treating everything around you as chess pieces, or bargaining chips—do you enjoy games like this?
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: This is our reality, one that florid words cannot conceal.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: If we want to be strong, independent, even sit as equals with the great powers at the same table, then we must play by the same rules.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Is this the most absurd part of it?
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Kjerag is not the only country that preaches virtue and goodness. Those politicians, too, play the part of leaders and teachers in their own country.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: They are hypocritical and deceitful, but they teach their people to be honest and sincere; they are avaricious and insatiable, but they teach their people to be gentle and kind.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: I ask, then, Sir Enciodes...
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Are the virtues that I preach to the people of Kjerag as Saintess, and the tenets of the scriptures, all lies and nonsense to you?
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Enya.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: The fire flickers as a charred piece of wood snaps into two.
The name, buried for so many years, is once again uttered by a Silverash.
Sir Enciodes.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Would you have come to me today, if I did not summon you with the authority of the Saintess?
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Kjerag faces an unprecedented crisis. One wrong step, and we may never recover.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: We need unity more than ever, and the authority of the Saintess is crucial at a time like this.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: I see you've finally recognized the importance of faith to our people.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: I never denied it.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Faith in Kjeragandr is fundamental to us. We cannot even begin to talk about this land without it.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Yes... more than one person has said as much to me.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: But when we view faith from the perspective of gain and loss, have we really paused to think if our history, free from the threat of Catastrophe, was a mere coincidence?
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: There is no point in discussing whether it was a miracle or not.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: What happened, happened. The only thing we can do now is to calm the people.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: On this, at least, we are in agreement.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: It's not an easy thing.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Then, I shall begin my Pilgrimage as Saintess.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: What?
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: I set out early tomorrow morning. The news will be known all across Kjerag by dusk.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Was this the Vine-Bead Court's decision?
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: It was my decision.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: The Pilgrimage takes place in spring or autumn. Has a Pilgrimage ever taken place in the middle of winter?
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Has a Catastrophe ever occurred in Kjerag?
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: We don't have time.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: You know how dangerous this is...
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Of course I do. But how else do we calm the people's fear of Catastrophe? How do we make them believe that Kjeragandr will forgive their 'transgressions'?
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: There will be no companion, no temporal possessions, no carriages, no rest.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: The Saintess shall circle the land of Kjerag on foot, atoning for the sins of all the people, and praying for Kjeragandr's forgiveness.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: It will take three months, if all goes well. More than one Saintess has died on the journey.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Then why do you—
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Brother.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: The young woman pauses for a moment, then continues like the snow melting into river, flowing down the peaks of Matterhorn.
They are surrounded by warmth, like those winter afternoon naps they took together when they were children.
The distant shadows of the past return, trying to find the children who have grown up, find traces of the past on their faces.
As Enya of Clan Silverash, I never forgave the choice that you made.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: As the Saintess of Kjerag, however, I am glad that there is still something I can do.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: I know... it is not just the guidance of faith that Kjerag needs. Indeed, misunderstandings about faith have hindered us for too long.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: The changes that you brought about, the factories, the railways, the trade... and the station that watches the sky, are all things that can lead our people out of the prison of the mind.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Kjerag needs change. You must not stop.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: What I must do is to convince the people that what is happening is not because they did anything wrong, that there is salvation from their sufferings.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: There is only one salvation in the minds of the people, and only one capable of carrying the burden...
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: I must fulfill my duty as Kjerag's Saintess.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: You summoned me to inform me of your decision.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Of course. I'm not asking for your opinion. You cannot stop me, nor do you have any reason to.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: I leave the matters of the three clans back to themselves while I am away from the Vine-Bead Court. Please make the necessary preparations to avoid any trouble.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: And...
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Take care of Ensia while I'm away. Don't leave her in fear.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Ensia isn't a child anymore. She won't be so easily frightened.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: I'm more concerned about—
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: It's getting late, Sir Enciodes. You should go and make preparations.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Is this an order to leave?
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: If you've understood it, you should act on it.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: As you bid.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: I take my leave.
attributes.name: Enciodes

attributes.content: Enciodes bows reverently to the Saintess and exits.
As he has done so, so many times before.
Mali, wait for me!
attributes.name: Kjerag Girl

attributes.content: The ball hits the wall of the farmhouse and bounces back to the feet of the boy, who waves at the girl trying to catch up to him.
Come on! You're always so slow.
attributes.name: Kjerag Boy

attributes.content: He kicks the ball several meters away. The girl grumbles and chases after it.

The children are getting some rare free time. In this foothill village with only a couple dozen households, the children, whose parents are too busy to watch, are playing in the center of the village.
As you know, the Saintess has begun a Pilgrimage for our people. This village is her final stop before she ascends the mountain.
attributes.name: Local Cleric

attributes.content: Remember what I told you, change into clean clothes, and do not disturb the Saintess when you see her. Am I clear?
attributes.name: Local Cleric

attributes.content: Will the Saintess really be able to summon Kjeragandr back to us, Father?
attributes.name: Eager Kjerag Villager

attributes.content: My wife is working in the city, and I haven't heard from her since the disaster. People in the nearby villages say Sir Enciodes has put a blockade around the scene, and no one knows what is happening.
attributes.name: Eager Kjerag Villager

attributes.content: Do not worry, Bauer. This is exactly why the Saintess is embarking on her journey. Kjeragandr will not abandon any of Her children.
attributes.name: Local Cleric

attributes.content: But all of us here in this village have already been abandoned, haven't we?
attributes.name: Elderly Kjerag Villager

attributes.content: None of the young folk have been willing to stay on the farms since Karlan Trade started their business. Let me tell you, if Bauer didn't go lame when he was little...
attributes.name: Elderly Kjerag Villager

attributes.content: Hey! Is this all you have to say, when the Father and I have been taking care of you since your son left for the city?
attributes.name: Eager Kjerag Villager

attributes.content: And I haven't heard a peep from him since the disaster. I'll be damned if I got anything else to say.
attributes.name: Elderly Kjerag Villager

attributes.content: Alright, alright, let's not argue in the Saintess's presence.
attributes.name: Local Cleric

attributes.content: Bauer, you should go check on old Carl. He said he wanted to catch a glimpse of the Saintess, even if he had to crawl on all fours. Help me talk him out of—
attributes.name: Local Cleric

attributes.content: (Shouting) I see the Saintess, Father! She'll be here soon.
attributes.name: Kjerag Village Lookout

attributes.content: What? It's much sooner than I expected... Alright. Let's go wait by the roadside. And brighten your faces!
attributes.name: Local Cleric

attributes.content: A few dozen villagers line the roadside, turning their faces to the distance, from whence the Saintess is approaching.

The cleric averts his eyes subconsciously, nervously rubbing his palms with his fingers. He was assigned to this village by the Vine-Bead Court twenty years ago, and has never seen the Saintess outside of portraits.
The footsteps approach. He focuses his entire attention on his facial muscles, hoping to leave a good impression on the Saintess.
Bam—
Oh—
attributes.name: Kjerag Boy

attributes.content: (Whisper) Mali, you fool! Look what you've done!
attributes.name: Kjerag Girl

attributes.content: What the... blast it, I forgot about you two.
attributes.name: Local Cleric

attributes.content: A-Are you alright, Saintess?
attributes.name: Local Cleric

attributes.content: Mali! Come and apologize to the Saintess.
attributes.name: Local Cleric

attributes.content: I-I'm sorry, Saintess.
attributes.name: Kjerag Boy

attributes.content: It's alright. Just a ball. It didn't hurt at all.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: I still have to continue the ritual. Can you go home? Are your parents here?
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: S-Saintess—
attributes.name: Local Cleric

attributes.content: Mom and Dad died a long time ago. My home is the temple.
attributes.name: Kjerag Boy

attributes.content: I'll, er, go home then, Father?
attributes.name: Kjerag Boy

attributes.content: (Under the breath) Go! I'll have you copying scriptures if you cause any more trouble!
attributes.name: Local Cleric

attributes.content: *sob*—
attributes.name: Kjerag Boy

attributes.content: I deeply apologize, Saintess...
attributes.name: Local Cleric

attributes.content: How many children like him are there in the village?
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: I've taken in nine. Some are orphans, while others were entrusted to me by their parents who went to the cities to find work.
attributes.name: Local Cleric

attributes.content: But we haven't heard from them since the disaster...
attributes.name: Local Cleric

attributes.content: ...Saintess! Do you know what's happening with this disaster? When can my wife come back?
attributes.name: Eager Kjerag Villager

attributes.content: What did I tell you, Bauer?
attributes.name: Local Cleric

attributes.content: Has Kjeragandr really abandoned us?
attributes.name: Eager Kjerag Villager

attributes.content: Enough—
attributes.name: Local Cleric

attributes.content: She would never do that.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: In these days of hardship, we must remember, believe, and trust that She is kind and just.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: I will do my best, so that your prayers, and the prayers of all Kjerag, will be heard by Her once again.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: Please, trust me, in Kjeragandr's name.
attributes.name: Enya

attributes.content: By Kjeragandr, She will protect us, and protect the Saintess.
attributes.name: Local Cleric

attributes.content: After a moment of silence, the villagers bow their heads together behind the cleric in a gesture of reverence. They have chosen to believe.
The Saintess nods and departs again. The snowy peaks in the distance await.
